MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
SARAH MCCRACKEN, Esq., TOBENER RAVENSCROFT LLP (415) 504-2165 smccracken@tobenerlaw.com Attorney for PLAINTIFFs
Elizabeth A. Evans, Esq.: CLARK HILL LLP (213) 891-9100 eevans@clarkhill.com Attorney for Defendants/Cross-Defendant, S4 DEVELOPMENT, LLC; SHARA COLETTA (erroneously sued and served as Shara Coleta); SEAN COLETTA (erroneously sued and served as Sean Colleta)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAMES CHIZINSKI'S, Motion To Compel Defendants Sean Coletta, Shara Coletta, And S4 Development, Llcs Further Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production, Set One; And Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff James Chizinski's motion to compel defendants Sean Coletta, Shara Coletta, and S4 Development (collectively "Defendants") to provide further supplemental responses to his Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One, and for sanctions, is granted.

Defendants shall provide verified amended responses within 30 calendar days of entry of this order. Defendants shall make an initial production and provide a privilege log within 30 calendar days of entry of this order. If the full production is voluminous, Defendants may make a reasonable rolling production but in no event shall delay the completion of their production beyond 60 days absent further order of this court.

Both parties seek sanctions. The relevant discovery statutes provide that the court shall order sanctions payable by the party who unsuccessfully opposed a motion to compel unless that party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (See CCP 2030.290(c); 2031.300(c).) The court does not find substantial justification or unjust circumstances. Defendants shall pay sanctions, for which they are jointly and severally liable, to Chizinski in the amount of $5,328 within 30 calendar days of entry of this order.

[SEE MINI MINUTES & WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS]

Order is signed.
